metadata.summary: Relational schema design for integrity, performance, and maintainability. Core rules: normalize to 3NF, use ULID/UUIDv7 PKs, explicit ON DELETE, created/updated timestamps, migrations via expand-contract pattern.

content.title: Database Design
content.summary: Relational schema design for integrity, performance, and maintainability. Core rules: normalize to 3NF first and denormalize only with a measured query showing the join is the bottleneck; use ULID or UUIDv7 primary keys (not auto-increment integers that leak row counts); every entity needs created_at and updated_at (timestamptz); foreign keys must have explicit ON DELETE (default RESTRICT, never implicit CASCADE).

why.p: Premature denormalization creates update-fanout consistency bugs. Auto-increment integer PKs leak business information (record count, growth rate). Missing ON DELETE defaults produce accidental mass deletes when a parent is removed. Migrations combining ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN NOT NULL DEFAULT on large tables cause production lock-outs; the expand-contract pattern eliminates this risk.

list.item: Greenfield schema for a new product (3NF first, denormalize with evidence later).
Major feature touching more than 2 tables — needs ER diagram + migration plan.
Performance regression traced to schema (missing index, wrong column type, hot row contention).
Move fast prototypes you will throw away in a week — SQLite + single table is fine.
Read-mostly content sites — a CMS or static generator covers it without a relational model.
Append-only event logs — use TimescaleDB or ClickHouse instead of fighting Postgres indexes.

rule.statement: Use ULID or UUID v7 primary keys, not auto-incrementing integers. Every entity table must have created_at and updated_at timestamptz columns defaulting to now().
rule.rationale: Auto-increment integers leak business information (record count, growth rate). UUID v4 has random distribution that fragments B-tree indexes. ULID/UUID v7 are time-ordered, maintaining insert locality while preventing enumeration.

rule.statement: Every foreign key must have an explicit ON DELETE action. Default to ON DELETE RESTRICT. Use CASCADE only when the child rows are logically part of the parent (e.g., order items when an order is deleted).
rule.rationale: Implicit ON DELETE NO ACTION defers the check but still raises on commit, surprising developers. RESTRICT is explicit intent. Accidental CASCADE on a chain of 3+ tables can wipe large portions of the database with a single accidental delete.

rule.statement: Create indexes based on actual query patterns. Review pg_stat_user_indexes.idx_scan quarterly and drop indexes with zero scans.
rule.rationale: Every index increases write overhead (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE must maintain all indexes). An unused index wastes I/O and storage with no read benefit.

rule.statement: Order composite index columns by selectivity: put the most selective column (or the column used in equality filters) first. Columns used only in ORDER BY go last.
rule.rationale: A composite index (status, created_at) supports WHERE status = 'pending' ORDER BY created_at but NOT WHERE created_at > ? ORDER BY status. The leading column must match the filter.

rule.statement: Use the expand-contract pattern for adding NOT NULL columns to large tables: add as nullable, backfill in batches, then add the NOT NULL constraint once 100% populated.
rule.rationale: A single ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN NOT NULL DEFAULT on a large Postgres table rewrites the entire table and holds an exclusive lock for the duration. This causes production downtime proportional to table size.

rule.statement: Every migration must have a reversible downgrade() function or be explicitly marked as irreversible with a comment explaining why.
rule.rationale: Without a down migration, rolling back a deployment requires manual intervention. Even irreversible data migrations (e.g., dropping a column) should have a stub that raises an explicit error rather than silently failing.

antipattern.description: God table: a single table with 50+ columns for loosely related entities.
antipattern.reason: Makes every query wide; most columns are NULL for any given row; adding indexes for different query patterns bloats write overhead. Normalize into focused tables and join when needed.

antipattern.description: Using FLOAT or DOUBLE for monetary amounts.
antipattern.reason: IEEE 754 floating-point cannot represent most decimal fractions exactly. Repeated arithmetic accumulates rounding errors. Always use NUMERIC(12,2) or BIGINT cents for money.

antipattern.description: Applying ON DELETE CASCADE to foreign keys without careful review of the deletion chain.
antipattern.reason: A cascade chain 3+ tables deep means a single parent delete wipes all descendant rows. A developer deleting a test user can wipe all their orders, payments, and shipments in a single query.

template.purpose: Reference e-commerce schema with UUIDs, constraints, indexes, soft-delete, audit trigger
Alembic migration with up/down using expand-contract pattern
